steps.title: Use the chemical signatures
steps.content: The passage sets up two pigment clues. Red ocher is mineral-based and gets its color from iron oxide, while plant-based pigments contain a high level of carbon. The tested red pigment had no iron and a high level of carbon.

steps.title: Keep the conclusion scoped
steps.content: Those results support a conclusion about the tested pigment on several beads, not about every Natufian bead or every reason the beadmakers chose a pigment. The evidence points away from red ocher and toward a plant-based pigment.

steps.title: Reject extra claims
steps.content: Choice B invents a preference based on brightness, but the passage never compares brightness. Choice C invents a reason about ocher being difficult to find. Choice D claims the beads are the oldest surviving examples, but the passage provides no comparison to other ancient beads. Choice A stays with the direct chemical evidence: some examined beads were decorated with plant-based pigment rather than ocher. The answer is A.
